REQ-MSG-001.description: Message.Topic must be a non-empty UTF-8 string identifying the publish destination.

REQ-MSG-002.description: Message.Payload must be a byte slice carrying the message body; nil or empty is valid.

REQ-MSG-003.description: Message.QoS must reflect the quality-of-service level negotiated for this delivery.

REQ-MSG-004.description: Message.Retained must be true when the broker is delivering a stored retained copy.

REQ-MSG-005.description: Message.PacketID must be non-zero for QoS >= 1 messages and zero for QoS 0.

REQ-V5-MSG-001.description: Message.ResponseTopic must carry the MQTT v5 Response Topic property (property ID 0x08).

REQ-V5-MSG-002.description: Message.CorrelationData must carry the MQTT v5 Correlation Data property (property ID 0x09).

REQ-V5-MSG-003.description: Message.UserProperties must carry MQTT v5 User Property key/value pairs (property ID 0x26).

REQ-V5-MSG-004.description: Message.ContentType must carry the MQTT v5 Content Type property (property ID 0x03).

REQ-V5-MSG-005.description: Message.ExpiryInterval must carry the MQTT v5 Message Expiry Interval in seconds (property ID 0x02); 0 means no expiry.

REQ-QOS-004.description: The three QoS constant values must be mutually distinct.

REQ-QOS2-001.description: An outbound QoS 2 Publish must send PUBLISH then complete only after the PUBLISH → PUBREC → PUBREL → PUBCOMP handshake succeeds.

REQ-QOS2-002.description: On receiving PUBREC for an in-flight outbound publish the client must send PUBREL; on PUBCOMP it must resolve the publish as complete.

REQ-QOS2-003.description: An inbound QoS 2 PUBLISH must be acknowledged with PUBREC and delivered to subscribers exactly once, on receipt of PUBREL, deduplicated by packet identifier.

REQ-QOS2-004.description: Each QoS 2 handshake step must fail with ErrTimeout if unanswered within the configured per-step timeout, and must honour context cancellation and client close.

REQ-WIRE-001.description: VarLen encoding must use 7-bit LSB groups with MSB=1 as the continuation marker (MQTT §2.2.3).

REQ-WIRE-002.description: VarLen encoding must represent values 0 to 268,435,455 in at most 4 bytes.

REQ-WIRE-003.description: VarLen decoding must return an error when a 5th byte with MSB=1 is encountered.

REQ-WIRE-004.description: String encoding must prefix UTF-8 bytes with a 2-byte big-endian byte count (MQTT §1.5.4).

REQ-WIRE-005.description: CONNECT must encode the protocol name as the 6-byte sequence 0x00 0x04 'M' 'Q' 'T' 'T'.

REQ-WIRE-006.description: CONNECT v3.1.1 must set the protocol level byte to 0x04.

REQ-WIRE-007.description: PUBLISH fixed header bit 0 must encode the RETAIN flag (MQTT §3.3.1.3).

REQ-WIRE-008.description: PUBLISH fixed header bits [2:1] must encode the QoS level (MQTT §3.3.1.2).

REQ-WIRE-009.description: PUBLISH fixed header bit 3 must encode the DUP flag (MQTT §3.3.1.1).

REQ-WIRE-010.description: QoS >= 1 PUBLISH packets must include a non-zero 2-byte big-endian packet identifier.

REQ-WIRE-011.description: SUBSCRIBE packets must include a non-zero 2-byte big-endian packet identifier.

REQ-WIRE-012.description: UNSUBSCRIBE packets must include a non-zero 2-byte big-endian packet identifier.

REQ-WIRE-013.description: PUBREC packets must carry the 2-byte big-endian packet identifier of the PUBLISH being acknowledged.

REQ-WIRE-014.description: PUBREL packets must use fixed-header flags 0b0010 and carry the 2-byte big-endian packet identifier.

REQ-WIRE-015.description: PUBCOMP packets must carry the 2-byte big-endian packet identifier completing the QoS 2 handshake.

REQ-CONN-001.description: Dial must send CONNECT to the broker before waiting for CONNACK.

REQ-CONN-002.description: Dial must not return the Client until a CONNACK packet is received.

REQ-CONN-003.description: Dial must return an error if the TCP connection cannot be established.

REQ-CONN-004.description: Dial must close the TCP connection if any part of the CONNECT/CONNACK handshake fails.

REQ-CONN-005.description: Dial (v3.1.1) must return an error when the CONNACK return code byte is non-zero.

REQ-CONN-006.description: Close must send a DISCONNECT packet before closing the TCP connection.

REQ-CONN-007.description: Close must close the TCP connection after sending DISCONNECT.

REQ-CONN-008.description: Close must be idempotent: subsequent calls after the first must not panic or produce side effects.

REQ-CONN-009.description: The client must send PINGREQ at the negotiated keepalive interval.

REQ-CONN-010.description: The keepalive goroutine must exit when Close is called.

REQ-CONN-011.description: WithWill must encode the will topic, will message, will QoS, and will retain flag in the CONNECT packet per MQTT §3.1.2.5–3.1.2.7.

REQ-TLS-001.description: When WithTLS is configured, Dial must wrap the TCP connection with tls.Client and complete the TLS handshake before sending CONNECT.

REQ-TLS-002.description: A failed TLS handshake (untrusted server certificate, or missing client certificate under mutual TLS) must cause Dial to return a non-nil error and not establish an MQTT session.

REQ-TLS-003.description: DialTLS must supply a default tls.Config (TLS 1.2 minimum, ServerName derived from the address) when none is provided, and an explicit WithTLS must take precedence over that default.

REQ-WS-001.description: DialWS must accept ws:// and wss:// URLs and reject any other scheme with an error.

REQ-WS-002.description: DialWS over wss:// must complete the TLS handshake (using WithTLS or a default TLS 1.2 config with ServerName from the URL host) before the WebSocket handshake.

REQ-WS-003.description: The WebSocket opening handshake must request the mqtt subprotocol and verify the 101 status and the RFC 6455 Sec-WebSocket-Accept value before use.

REQ-WS-004.description: Outbound MQTT control packets must be sent as masked WebSocket binary frames (RFC 6455 §5.3), with correct 7/16/64-bit payload length encoding.

REQ-WS-005.description: Inbound WebSocket data frames must be unmasked and reassembled into the MQTT byte stream; the 16- and 64-bit extended length forms must be decoded.

REQ-WS-006.description: A WebSocket ping must be answered with a pong, and a close frame must terminate the stream; Close must send a close frame before closing the connection.

REQ-PUB-001.description: Publish must return ErrTopicEmpty when topic is an empty string.

REQ-PUB-002.description: Publish must return ErrQoSUnsupported when QoS is ExactlyOnce (QoS 2).

REQ-PUB-003.description: Publish must return ErrClosed when called on a closed client.

REQ-PUB-004.description: Publish must return ctx.Err() when the context is cancelled before the send completes.

REQ-PUB-005.description: Publish at QoS 0 must not include a packet identifier in the PUBLISH packet.

REQ-PUB-006.description: Publish at QoS 1 must include a non-zero packet identifier in the PUBLISH packet.

REQ-SUB-001.description: Subscribe must return ErrTopicEmpty when the topic filter is an empty string.

REQ-SUB-002.description: Subscribe must return ErrClosed when called on a closed client.

REQ-SUB-003.description: Subscribe must return a Subscription backed by a buffered message channel.

REQ-SUB-004.description: The default subscription channel depth must be at least 64 when not configured by the caller.

REQ-SUB-005.description: Subscription channel depth must be configurable by the caller via SubscriberOption.

REQ-SUB-006.description: Subscribe must send a SUBSCRIBE packet to the broker carrying the given filter and QoS.

REQ-SUB-007.description: Incoming PUBLISH messages must be delivered to all subscriptions where MatchTopic returns true.

REQ-SUB-008.description: A PUBLISH matching multiple active subscriptions must be delivered independently to each one.

REQ-WILD-001.description: MatchTopic("#") must return true for any topic that does not start with '$'.

REQ-WILD-002.description: MatchTopic("#") must return false for any topic that starts with '$'.

REQ-WILD-003.description: MatchTopic("prefix/#") must return true when topic equals "prefix" exactly.

REQ-WILD-004.description: MatchTopic("prefix/#") must return true when topic starts with "prefix/".

REQ-WILD-005.description: MatchTopic("prefix/#") must return false for '$'-prefixed topics when prefix has no '$'.

REQ-WILD-006.description: MatchTopic with '+' at any position must match exactly one topic level.

REQ-WILD-007.description: MatchTopic with '+' at level 0 must return false for topics that start with '$'.

REQ-WILD-008.description: MatchTopic must return true when filter equals topic and neither contains a wildcard character.

REQ-SAFETY-001.description: Client.Publish on a closed client must return ErrClosed.

REQ-SAFETY-002.description: Client.Subscribe on a closed client must return ErrClosed.

REQ-SAFETY-003.description: Client.Publish and Client.Subscribe must not panic on any valid or invalid input.

REQ-SAFETY-004.description: Client.Publish with an empty topic must return ErrTopicEmpty.

REQ-SAFETY-005.description: Client.Subscribe with an empty topic must return ErrTopicEmpty.

REQ-SAFETY-006.description: All active subscription channels must be closed when the TCP connection is lost.

REQ-SAFETY-007.description: All active subscription channels must be closed when Client.Close is called.

REQ-SAFETY-008.description: Message delivery must never block: a full subscription channel must silently drop the message.

REQ-MOCK-001.description: Broker.Subscribe must deliver all retained messages matching the filter at subscribe time.

REQ-MOCK-002.description: Broker.route must store a retained message (non-empty payload) before fan-out to current subscribers.

REQ-MOCK-003.description: Broker.route must delete a stored retained message when the published payload is empty.

REQ-MOCK-004.description: Broker.route must deliver to all subscriptions where MatchTopic returns true for the message topic.

REQ-MOCK-005.description: Broker.Dial must return a new client that is connected to the same in-process broker.

REQ-V5-CONN-001.description: v5.Dial must send an MQTT 5.0 CONNECT packet (protocol level byte 0x05).

REQ-V5-CONN-002.description: v5.Dial must not return the Client until a CONNACK is received.

REQ-V5-CONN-003.description: v5.Dial must return an error when the CONNACK reason code byte is non-zero.

REQ-V5-CONN-004.description: v5 Client must parse and apply TopicAliasMax from CONNACK properties.

REQ-V5-WIRE-001.description: Each MQTT v5 property must be preceded by its 1-byte property identifier.

REQ-V5-WIRE-002.description: The v5 property section must be preceded by a VarLen total byte count of all property bytes.

REQ-V5-WIRE-003.description: v5 property decoding must skip unknown property identifiers without returning an error.

REQ-V5-WIRE-004.description: CONNECT v5.0 must set the protocol level byte to 0x05.

REQ-V5-PUB-001.description: PublishV5 must encode each UserProperty pair with property identifier 0x26.

REQ-V5-PUB-002.description: PublishV5 must encode ResponseTopic with property identifier 0x08 when non-empty.

REQ-V5-PUB-003.description: PublishV5 must encode CorrelationData with property identifier 0x09 when non-nil and non-empty.

REQ-V5-PUB-004.description: PublishV5 must encode MessageExpiryInterval with property identifier 0x02 when non-zero.

REQ-V5-PUB-005.description: PublishV5 must encode ContentType with property identifier 0x03 when non-empty.

REQ-V5-PUB-006.description: PublishV5 must encode TopicAlias with property identifier 0x23 when non-zero.

REQ-V5-SUB-001.description: SubscribeV5 must encode QoS in bits [1:0] of the subscription options byte.

REQ-V5-SUB-002.description: SubscribeV5 must set bit 2 of the subscription options byte when NoLocal is true.

REQ-V5-SUB-003.description: SubscribeV5 must set bit 3 of the subscription options byte when RetainAsPublished is true.

REQ-V5-SUB-004.description: SubscribeV5 must encode RetainHandling in bits [5:4] of the subscription options byte.

REQ-V5-ALIAS-001.description: v5 Client must register a topic alias when PUBLISH arrives with both a non-empty topic and a non-zero alias.

REQ-V5-ALIAS-002.description: v5 Client must resolve a topic alias when PUBLISH arrives with empty topic and a non-zero alias.

REQ-V5-ALIAS-003.description: v5 Client must drop a PUBLISH when topic is empty and the alias is not registered.

REQ-V5-SESSION-001.description: WithSessionExpiry must encode SessionExpiryInterval with property identifier 0x11 in CONNECT properties.

REQ-CONC-001.description: All exported Client methods must be safe for concurrent use from multiple goroutines (go test -race must pass).

REQ-CONC-002.description: Concurrent calls to Publish and Subscribe on the same Client must not cause a data race.

REQ-CONC-003.description: Concurrent calls to Close and any other Client method must not panic.

REQ-LEAK-001.description: All goroutines started by Dial must exit after Close is called; no goroutine leak.

REQ-LEAK-002.description: Subscription state (channel, filter) must be released when a Subscription is closed.

REQ-LEAK-003.description: Message payload bytes delivered to a Subscription channel must be a copy owned by the receiver; internal buffers must not be aliased.

REQ-ORDER-001.description: Messages on a single Subscription channel must arrive in the order they were received from the network.

REQ-ORDER-002.description: QoS 0 PUBLISH packets sent from a single goroutine must be written to the network in the order Publish was called.

REQ-FAULT-001.description: A broker sending a malformed variable-length integer must cause the client to close without panicking.

REQ-FAULT-002.description: A broker sending an unexpected or unknown packet type must be silently ignored without corrupting client state.

REQ-FAULT-003.description: TCP connection loss must cause all subscription channels to be closed within the next read attempt.

REQ-FAULT-004.description: Publish on a client after Close() must return ErrClosed (session loss: publish path).

REQ-FAULT-005.description: Subscribe on a client after Close() must return ErrClosed (session loss: subscribe path).

REQ-FAULT-006.description: Close() must be idempotent: calling it multiple times must not panic or return an error.

REQ-FAULT-007.description: Publish and Subscribe with an empty topic must return ErrTopicEmpty before any broker contact.

REQ-FAULT-008.description: Publish with a cancelled context must return a non-nil error (context cancellation / packet loss).

REQ-FAULT-009.description: When a subscription channel is full, the incoming message must be dropped silently; Publish must not block or error (DropNewest back-pressure).

REQ-FAULT-010.description: Concurrent calls to Close() and Publish() must not panic or deadlock.

REQ-RELAY-001.description: SpecVersion constant must equal "0.3" and match relay.SpecVersion (RELAY spec §17.12).

REQ-RELAY-002.description: ErrClosed, ErrNotConnected, ErrPayloadTooLarge must each wrap the corresponding relay sentinel so errors.Is reaches relay.ErrClosed etc. (RELAY spec §5.2).

REQ-RELAY-003.description: ErrTimeout must be defined and must wrap relay.ErrTimeout (RELAY spec §5.1).

REQ-RELAY-004.description: BackPressurePolicy must define DropNewest=0, DropOldest=1, Block=2 (RELAY spec §14.1).

REQ-RELAY-005.description: SubscriberConfig must include a BackPressure field of type BackPressurePolicy defaulting to DropNewest (RELAY spec §14.1).

REQ-RELAY-006.description: WithBackPressure must return a SubscriberOption that sets BackPressure on SubscriberConfig (RELAY spec §14.1).

REQ-RELAY-007.description: Adapt must wrap a Client as relay.Node returning relay.MQTT as the Protocol (RELAY spec §10.3).

REQ-RELAY-008.description: Message.ToMessage must return a relay.Message with Protocol=relay.MQTT, ID=Topic, and mqtt.qos/mqtt.retained in Meta (RELAY spec §15.7.4).

REQ-RELAY-009.description: FromMessage must set Message.Topic from relay.Message.ID and parse mqtt.qos/mqtt.retained from Meta (RELAY spec §15.7.4).

REQ-RELAY-010.description: HealthStatus must define HealthOK=0, HealthDegraded=1, HealthDown=2 matching RELAY spec §9.

REQ-RELAY-011.description: HealthProvider interface must expose Health() returning a Health struct with Status and Details (RELAY spec §9).

REQ-RELAY-012.description: Metrics struct must carry WriteCount, DeliverCount, DropCount, BytesWritten, BytesDelivered, ErrorCount (RELAY spec §9).

REQ-RELAY-013.description: MetricsProvider interface must expose Metrics() returning a Metrics struct (RELAY spec §9).

REQ-RELAY-014.description: Drainer interface must expose CloseWithDrain(ctx context.Context) error that blocks until in-flight messages drain (RELAY spec §9).

REQ-VISSR-001.description: PathToTopic must convert a VSS dot path to an MQTT topic, mapping a trailing .* subtree to /# and a * segment to +.

REQ-VISSR-002.description: TopicToPath must convert an MQTT slash topic back to a VSS dot path (inverse of PathToTopic for concrete topics).

REQ-VISSR-003.description: Signal must carry Path, Value, and Timestamp with VISSv2-compatible JSON field names.

REQ-VISSR-004.description: Signal.Float, Signal.Bool, and Signal.String must return the typed value with an ok flag that is false when the underlying value is a different type.

REQ-VISSR-005.description: vissr.New must wrap an mqtt.Client and vissr.Client must delegate transport to it.

REQ-VISSR-006.description: Set/SetFloat/SetBool/SetString must JSON-encode a Signal envelope and publish it to the mapped MQTT topic, returning ErrTopicEmpty for an empty path.

REQ-VISSR-007.description: Subscribe must map the VSS path to an MQTT filter and return a Subscription delivering decoded Signals, returning ErrTopicEmpty for an empty path.

REQ-VISSR-008.description: Client.Close must close the underlying mqtt.Client.

REQ-VISSR-009.description: A malformed (non-JSON) payload must be dropped without stalling the Signal stream; a missing path must be backfilled from the topic.

REQ-VISSR-010.description: Subscription.Unsubscribe and Subscription.Close must delegate to the underlying mqtt.Subscription.

REQ-FED-001.description: A federation Route must declare source topic Filters and an optional MaxQoS cap, StripPrefix, and AddPrefix.

REQ-FED-002.description: New must create a Bridge forwarding from a source to a destination mqtt.Client; Pair must create two Bridges with independent per-direction route sets.

REQ-FED-003.description: Start must subscribe to every route filter on the source; on a subscribe error it must roll back prior subscriptions and return the error. Start must be idempotent.

REQ-FED-004.description: Forwarded messages must be republished to the destination at min(message QoS, route MaxQoS).

REQ-FED-005.description: Topic remapping must strip StripPrefix (on a topic-level boundary) and prepend AddPrefix before republishing; a topic that reduces to empty must not be republished.

REQ-FED-006.description: Stop must end forwarding and close all source subscriptions without closing the source or destination clients; it must be idempotent. The Bridge must not reconnect automatically (RELAY spec §6.10).

REQ-FED-007.description: Stats must report the number of messages forwarded and dropped.

REQ-FED-008.description: A republish failure must increment the dropped counter and must not stop the forwarding goroutine.

REQ-REST-001.description: rest.New must wrap an mqtt.Client and Handler must serve POST /publish, GET /subscribe, and GET /retain routes with multi-level topic/filter path segments.

REQ-REST-002.description: POST /publish/{topic} must publish the request body to the topic and return 202 Accepted; a body exceeding the configured maximum must return 413.

REQ-REST-003.description: The optional ?qos query parameter must select the publish QoS (0, 1, or 2); an out-of-range or non-numeric value must return 400.

REQ-REST-004.description: GET /subscribe/{filter} must subscribe to the filter and stream matching messages as Server-Sent Events with Content-Type text/event-stream.

REQ-REST-005.description: Each forwarded SSE event must be a JSON-encoded mqtt.Message prefixed with 'data: ' and flushed immediately.

REQ-REST-006.description: The SSE stream must terminate and unsubscribe when the request context is cancelled or the subscription channel closes.

REQ-REST-007.description: GET /retain/{topic} must return the retained message for the topic within the configured timeout, or 404 if none arrives.

REQ-REST-008.description: GET /retain/{topic} must ignore live (non-retained) traffic and only return a message whose Retained flag is set.
